title: The Monster

evidence.note: Published 1925; the 95-year US copyright term has expired for works published through 1930, so the film print is public domain in the US by term expiry.

US Copyright Office public records (1978+ renewals/assignments)

editorial: The Monster is a 1925 American silent horror-comedy directed by Roland West and released by Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer, starring Lon Chaney as the mad Dr. Ziska presiding over an isolated sanitarium. Adapted from Crane Wilbur's stage play, it is an early 'old dark house' picture and an influential template for later mad-scientist horror. As a 1925 US publication, the film print is public domain by expiry of the 95-year US copyright term; other layers remain undetermined.
